01_initial_request.input_data: Sordaria macrospora strains (wild-type and Δsci1 mutant), codon-optimized TurboID sequence, SCI1 gene, plasmids, and biotin
01_initial_request.user_intent: To establish the BioID proximity labeling method in the filamentous fungus Sordaria macrospora and identify proximal proteins of the SmSTRIPAK complex component SCI1
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Identification of known SmSTRIPAK components (PRO11, SmMOB3, PRO22, SmPP2Ac1) with an enrichment factor ≥4 and FDR = 0.01 in LC-MS analysis

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] BioID method has been established in animal cells, plants and yeast but not yet in filamentous fungi. TurboID is an improved biotin ligase with faster labeling kinetics. [Gap] TurboID sequence needs to be adapted for S. macrospora expression. [Decision] To achieve proper expression in S. macrospora, chose to use codon optimization based on S. macrospora codon usage table to perform sequence adaptation on TurboID biotin ligase, expecting to obtain a codon-optimized TurboID sequence suitable for fungal expression.

parameters.reference: codon usage table of S. macrospora

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Codon-optimized TurboID sequence generated according to S. macrospora codon usage

references.relevance: Provides codon usage table for S. macrospora used for optimization

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Codon-optimized TurboID sequence is available. [Gap] Multiple expression constructs need to be generated for testing different promoters and fusion configurations. [Decision] To achieve flexible expression control, chose to use molecular cloning to perform plasmid generation with different promoters (ccg1, Smxyl, native sci1) and linker configurations on TurboID and SCI1-TurboID constructs, expecting to obtain multiple plasmids for transformation.

parameters.promoters: ccg1 constitutive promoter, xylose inducible Smxyl promoter, native sci1 promoter

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Multiple plasmids generated for SCI1-TurboID fusion protein and unfused TurboID control with different promoter configurations

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Plasmids for BioID constructs are ready. [Gap] S. macrospora strains need to express these constructs. [Decision] To achieve protein expression in fungal cells, chose to use fungal transformation to perform strain generation on wild-type and Δsci1 mutant S. macrospora strains, expecting to obtain transformed strains expressing TurboID constructs.

parameters.plasmids: SCI1-TurboID fusion and unfused TurboID control plasmids

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Transformed strains obtained expressing either SCI1-TurboID fusion protein or unfused TurboID ligase

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Transformed strains are available. [Gap] Protein expression levels need to be verified. [Decision] To achieve expression validation, chose to use Western blot with anti-HA antibody to perform protein detection on crude protein extracts from transformed strains, expecting to obtain confirmation of TurboID construct expression at expected molecular masses.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Free TurboID and SCI1-TurboID fusion proteins detected at expected molecular masses (38.5 kDa and 71.6 kDa respectively)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Protein expression is confirmed. [Gap] Functional activity of SCI1-TurboID fusion needs validation. [Decision] To achieve functional validation, chose to use complementation assay to perform fertility restoration test on Δsci1 mutant strains expressing SCI1-TurboID, expecting to obtain restoration of fruiting body development indicating functional fusion protein.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Both deletion strains expressing TurboID-tagged sci1 gene were complemented and developed mature fruiting bodies, indicating SCI1-TurboID fusion proteins can fulfill endogenous function of SCI1

references.relevance: Previous analysis showing sci1 deletion causes sterility

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Functional complementation is confirmed. [Gap] TurboID ligase activity needs to be verified in S. macrospora. [Decision] To achieve activity validation, chose to use Streptavidin-HRP detection to perform biotinylation assay on protein extracts from strains grown with biotin supplementation, expecting to obtain detection of biotinylated proteins indicating TurboID activity.

parameters.media: BMM complex medium and SWG synthetic medium
parameters.growth_conditions: 3 days in BMM or 5 days in SWG at 27°C

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Overexpression of free TurboID resulted in strong biotinylation upon boosting. SCI1-TurboID under native promoter showed wild-type-like biotinylation levels. 30 min boosting showed higher biotinylation than 10 min.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] TurboID activity is confirmed. [Gap] Optimal biotin concentration for BioID experiments needs to be determined. [Decision] To achieve optimal labeling conditions, chose to use biotin concentration testing to perform medium optimization on S. macrospora growth with varying biotin concentrations, expecting to obtain suitable biotin concentration that supports full life cycle with minimal background biotinylation.

parameters.criteria: full complementation of life cycle (discharged ascospores after 7-9 days), low biotin concentration

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 50 nM biotin in SWG medium selected as optimal condition supporting full life cycle with low background biotinylation

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Optimal growth conditions are established. [Gap] BioID proximity labeling experiment needs to be performed. [Decision] To achieve proximity labeling, chose to use BioID labeling protocol to perform biotin boost on Δsci1::p5'-sci1-L-TurboIDect strains grown in SWG with 50 nM biotin, expecting to obtain biotinylated proximal proteins for enrichment.

parameters.biotin_boost: 10 μM biotin for 30 min at RT
parameters.control_dilution: 1:6 with wild-type extract to match biotinylation levels

02_agent_trajectory.observation: BioID labeling performed with four biological replicates for both experimental and control strains

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] BioID labeling is complete. [Gap] Biotinylated proteins need to be enriched from cell lysates. [Decision] To achieve protein enrichment, chose to use Strep-Tactin Sepharose affinity purification to perform biotinylated protein capture from denatured cell lysates, expecting to obtain enriched biotinylated proteins for LC-MS analysis.

parameters.incubation: 30 min at RT on rotating wheel

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Biotinylated proteins enriched and eluted from Strep-Tactin Sepharose beads

references.relevance: Protocol for biotin affinity purification using Strep-Tactin Sepharose

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Biotinylated proteins are enriched. [Gap] Proteins need to be prepared for mass spectrometry. [Decision] To achieve peptide preparation, chose to use tryptic in-gel digestion with C18 STAGE tip purification to perform sample preparation on enriched protein eluates, expecting to obtain purified peptides for LC-MS analysis.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Peptides obtained through tryptic in-gel digestion and C18 STAGE tip purification

references.relevance: Protocol for tryptic in-gel digestion and C18 STAGE tip purification

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Peptides are prepared. [Gap] Proteins need to be identified and quantified. [Decision] To achieve protein identification, chose to use LC-MS analysis to perform mass spectrometry on purified peptides, expecting to obtain raw mass spectrometry data for protein identification.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: LC-MS raw data files generated for protein identification and quantification

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] LC-MS raw data is available. [Gap] Raw data needs to be processed for protein identification and statistical analysis. [Decision] To achieve data analysis, chose to use MaxQuant software to perform peptide identification and protein quantification on LC-MS raw files, expecting to obtain protein groups with LFQ intensities and statistical values.

parameters.database: S. macrospora specific proteomics dataset Smacospora_v93_peptides.fasta
parameters.variable_modifications: Oxidation of methionine, N-terminal acetylation, Phosphorylation (STY), biotinylation of lysines

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Protein groups identified with LFQ intensities, MS/MS counts, and statistical values. Total of 1185 proteins identified.

references.relevance: MaxQuant software for peptide identification and proteome-wide protein quantification
S. macrospora proteomics dataset used for database search

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] MaxQuant output is available. [Gap] Statistical analysis and enrichment calculation needs to be performed. [Decision] To achieve statistical validation, chose to use Perseus software to perform differential enrichment analysis on protein groups data, expecting to obtain significantly enriched proteins with enrichment factors and p-values.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Significantly enriched proteins identified with enrichment factor ≥4. SmSTRIPAK components PRO11, SmMOB3, PRO22, and SmPP2Ac1 found among top hits. Mean Pearson correlations: 0.9556 among experimental replicates, 0.9676 among control replicates.

references.relevance: Perseus computational platform for comprehensive analysis of proteomics data

03_success_verification.validation_technique: LC-MS based proximity proteomics with BioID/TurboID

enrichment_factor.interpretation: Threshold for significantly enriched proteins co-localizing with SCI1-L-TurboID

FDR.interpretation: Statistical significance threshold for protein identification

proteins_identified.interpretation: Total number of proteins identified in the BioID experiment

known_STRIPAK_components.interpretation: Known SmSTRIPAK components identified among top hits with enrichment factor ≥4

03_success_verification.final_verdict: SUCCESS - The research objective was achieved. BioID proximity labeling method was successfully established in S. macrospora using TurboID fused to SCI1. All quantifiable goals were met: known SmSTRIPAK components (PRO11, SmMOB3, PRO22, SmPP2Ac1) were identified with enrichment factor ≥4 and FDR = 0.01 in LC-MS analysis. The SCI1-TurboID fusion protein was functional (complemented Δsci1 sterility) and showed appropriate biotinylation activity. High reproducibility was demonstrated with Pearson correlations >0.95 among replicates.
